title: Rights During Detention
summary: Detention by police is strictly regulated and time-bound under law.

plainExplanation.whatItMeans: Police cannot keep you in custody indefinitely without court permission.
plainExplanation.whyItMatters: Unlawful detention violates the right to personal liberty.

whatPoliceMustDo: Produce detainee before magistrate within 24 hours

exampleScenario.situation: A person is kept in police station for 3 days without court order.
exampleScenario.explanation: Detention beyond 24 hours without magistrate approval is illegal.

whatToDoIfViolated: Approach magistrate or human rights commission
